title: Sentiment Analysis
concept: Sentiment analysis is a text classification task that flags positive, negative, or neutral tones in reviews and social media posts.

exercises.prompt: What does Sentiment Analysis help anwseriousness:
exercises.explain: Sentiment analysis is a type of text classification where we label text based on its emotional attitude (positive, negative, or neutral).

cards.title: Reading the Room
cards.body: Every day, millions of reviews, tweets, and comments are posted online. How do big companies know if people love or hate their new products without reading every single message? They use AI to automatically detect the emotional vibe of the text!

cards.title: What is Sentiment Analysis?
cards.body: Sentiment analysis is a way for AI to read a piece of text and figure out if the writer is feeling happy (positive), sad/angry (negative), or indifferent (neutral). It is a form of text classification that groups words by their emotional tone.

cards.title: The Review Boxes
cards.body: Imagine you are a restaurant owner reading comment cards. You sort them into three boxes: a smiley face box (positive), a frowny face box (negative), and a straight face box (neutral). Sentiment analysis is like having a super-fast assistant sort thousands of these digital cards into those three boxes in seconds!

cards.title: Sentiment in Action
cards.body: If a user posts: "This new game is amazing, I played it for hours!", the AI analyzes the words and labels it as **Positive**. If another user posts: "The game keeps crashing, I want a refund!", the AI labels it as **Negative**.

cards.title: The Sarcasm Trap
cards.body: People think AI sentiment analysis is 100% perfect. In reality, AI often struggles with sarcasm! If someone writes, "Oh great, another delay. Just what I wanted," a basic AI might see "great" and "what I wanted" and think it is a positive review, even though it's actually negative.

cards.title: Positive vs. Negative
cards.body: Look at how the AI classifies the tone of different sentences. It doesn't just look for words, it looks at the overall feeling.

left.text: 'The interface is super clean and easy to use!'

right.text: 'I hate how confusing the new menu layout is.'

exercises.prompt: If an AI reads the comment: "The food took two hours to arrive and was cold!", what sentiment will it most likely assign?
exercises.explain: The comment expresses frustration and disappointment, which are clear indicators of a negative sentiment.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Sentiment analysis only works by counting the number of happy or sad words in a sentence.
exercises.explain: Sentiment analysis goes beyond counting words; it uses machine learning to understand the context and overall feeling of the sentence.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the sentiment category.
exercises.sentence: A review saying 'The box contains four blue pens' has a _ sentiment because it has no emotional tone.
exercises.explain: When a statement only states objective facts without expressing happiness or anger, the sentiment is classified as neutral.

exercises.prompt: Which of these text inputs would an AI classify as NEUTRAL?
exercises.explain: The second option is a statement of fact with no emotional language, making it a neutral statement. The other options contain clear emotional words like 'love' and 'horrible'.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following best describes how Sentiment Analysis differs from simple keyword searching?

options.text: It identifies the emotional tone (positive, negative, neutral) of a text.
It only checks if a specific keyword exists in a dictionary.
It automatically corrects spelling and grammar mistakes.
It translates text from one language to another.

exercises.explain: Sentiment analysis determines the emotional tone behind words (positive, negative, or neutral), while keyword matching only looks for the presence of specific words without understanding the feeling behind them.
